articles.title: First-Time Homebuyer Inspection Checklist: 25 Things to Look For
articles.excerpt: Buying your first home? Use this step-by-step inspection checklist to know what your inspector will look at and what you should pay attention to before closing.
articles.body: As a first-time homebuyer, the inspection can feel overwhelming. This detailed checklist walks you through the exterior, roof, foundation, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, interior finishes, and safety items your inspector will evaluate. For each category, we explain what is considered normal wear versus a potential red flag that may require repair, negotiation, or further specialist evaluation...

articles.title: First-Time Homebuyer Pre-Inspection Checklist: Be Ready Before Your Inspector Arrives
articles.excerpt: Prepare your home inspection like a pro with this first-time homebuyer checklist covering documents, utilities, and on-site questions to ask.
articles.body: Preparation is key to getting the most value from your home inspection. This checklist covers what to bring (notebook, tape measure, inspection contingency), how to confirm utilities are on, what questions to ask about major systems, and how to organize photos and notes for your records. We also include a printable PDF you can take to the inspection...

articles.title: Radon Testing 101: What Homebuyers in High-Risk Areas Need to Know
articles.excerpt: Radon is a colorless, odorless gas that can affect any home. Learn when radon testing is recommended, how it works, and what your results mean.
articles.body: Radon is the second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking, and many regions of the country are considered high risk. In this guide, we explain how radon enters a home, how short-term and long-term tests work, what inspectors do during a professional radon test, and how mitigation systems can reduce levels...

articles.title: Inspection Tips for Multi-Unit and Apartment Buildings
articles.excerpt: Buying a duplex, triplex, or small apartment building? Here’s how multi-unit inspections differ from single-family inspections.
articles.body: Multi-unit properties introduce unique inspection considerations: shared systems, common areas, life-safety requirements, and tenant access coordination. This article outlines how inspectors assess representative samples of units, evaluate parking and exterior circulation, and document unit-specific versus building-wide issues...

articles.title: Are Same-Day Digital Inspection Reports Worth It?
articles.excerpt: Under contract on a tight timeline? See how same-day digital reports can keep your home purchase on track.
articles.body: In competitive markets, buyers may have only a few days to complete inspections and respond. Same-day digital reports help by delivering photos, summaries, and recommendations within hours of the inspection. We explain typical turnaround times, quality considerations, and when upgrading to same-day delivery makes sense...

articles.title: Termite and Wood-Destroying Organism Inspections: A Buyer’s Guide
articles.excerpt: Learn what termite inspectors look for, how reports are structured, and what happens if activity is found.
articles.body: In many regions, lenders or purchase agreements require a termite or WDO (wood-destroying organism) inspection. This article explains how inspectors assess accessible structural components, common signs of active or previous infestation, and how recommendations for treatment or structural repair are presented...

articles.title: Comparing Inspection Packages: Basic vs. Standard vs. Premium
articles.excerpt: Not sure which inspection package to choose? We break down what’s included at each level so you can match features to your budget.
articles.body: We typically offer three levels of inspection packages. The Basic package covers core structural and system components. The Standard package adds a same-day PDF report and limited thermal imaging. The Premium package bundles radon and mold testing at a discount. This article includes a side-by-side comparison chart and examples of which package is best for different buyer scenarios...

articles.title: Condo and Townhome Inspections: What’s Different from Single-Family Homes?
articles.excerpt: If you’re buying a condo or townhome, your inspection will focus on different elements than a detached home.
articles.body: In shared-wall communities, inspectors concentrate on the interior of the unit, limited exterior components, and visible portions of common areas. This guide explains what’s typically included, what falls under the HOA’s responsibility, and how to review HOA documents side-by-side with the inspection report...

articles.title: Annual Home Maintenance Checklist for New Owners
articles.excerpt: Keep your new home in top shape with this seasonal maintenance checklist.
articles.body: After closing, regular maintenance is essential to protect your investment. We provide a quarter-by-quarter checklist that covers roofs, gutters, grading, HVAC filters, caulking, and safety devices. Following this schedule can also reduce surprises at future inspections...

articles.title: Understanding Our Service Areas and Travel Fees
articles.excerpt: See how we define our core service areas, nearby covered cities, and when a travel fee may apply.
articles.body: Our scheduling tool uses your ZIP code to determine coverage and any applicable travel fees. This article explains how we group ZIP codes into service-area cities, when nearby covered cities are suggested for out-of-area addresses, and what typical travel fees look like for urban versus rural locations...

inspection_add_ons.name: Radon Testing
inspection_add_ons.description: Short-term radon test with calibrated electronic monitor, including written results and EPA guidance.

inspection_add_ons.name: Termite / WDO Inspection
inspection_add_ons.description: Inspection for termites and other wood-destroying organisms, with state-compliant reporting forms.

inspection_add_ons.name: Sewer Scope Inspection
inspection_add_ons.description: Camera inspection of the main sewer line from the home to the street or septic connection.

inspection_add_ons.name: Mold Sampling and Air Quality Testing
inspection_add_ons.description: Targeted surface and/or air sampling for suspected mold, with lab analysis and report.

inspection_add_ons.name: Same-Day Digital Report Delivery
inspection_add_ons.description: Expedited processing to deliver your full inspection report digitally by 9 PM the same day.

inspection_add_ons.name: Infrared Thermal Imaging Scan
inspection_add_ons.description: Thermal imaging survey to help identify hidden moisture, missing insulation, and overheating electrical components.

inspection_add_ons.name: Pool and Spa Inspection
inspection_add_ons.description: Inspection of accessible pool and spa components, including structure, equipment, and safety features.

inspection_add_ons.name: Limited Re-Inspection of Repairs
inspection_add_ons.description: Follow-up inspection of previously identified issues after repairs are completed.

inspection_services.name: Standard Home Buyer Inspection
inspection_services.description: Full inspection of a single-family home’s major systems and components, ideal for most buyers.

inspection_services.name: Condo / Townhome Inspection
inspection_services.description: Inspection of an individual condo or townhome unit, focusing on interiors and limited common elements.

inspection_services.name: Multi-Unit / Apartment Building Inspection
inspection_services.description: Comprehensive inspection for duplexes, triplexes, fourplexes, and small apartment buildings.

inspection_services.name: Pre-Listing Seller Inspection
inspection_services.description: Inspection for sellers who want to identify issues before listing a home on the market.

inspection_services.name: Annual Home Maintenance Inspection
inspection_services.description: A lighter-weight inspection focused on maintenance and safety for current homeowners.

inspectors.name: Henry Clark

inspectors.name: Ivy Thomas

inspectors.name: Luis Mendez
inspectors.bio: Rural-market inspector accustomed to longer travel distances and well/septic systems.
